assumptions: The developer has arrived after discovery.
The developer has Git, GitHub access, JDK 11 or later, Maven, and an IDE for the documented Selenium Java candidate.
The application type, live versus automated mode, framework, language, and browser or device target have not been selected.

developer_goal: Create a Sauce Labs trial account and, if Selenium Java is selected, run the linked example test through its first visible Sauce Labs result.

first_success.outcome: DesktopTests runs on Sauce Labs and its completed job and assets appear in Automated Test Results.
first_success.observable_signal: The completed job appears in Automated Test Results with its result and assets.

route_selection.surface: Sauce Labs free-trial Selenium Java DesktopTests
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Sauce Labs free-trial Selenium Java DesktopTests
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Selenium automated web test
candidates.reason: The guide is a complete seven-part test pattern, but Sauce Labs recommends WebdriverIO for JavaScript and also documents Java, C#, Python, and Ruby variants.

candidates.name: Live web or mobile test
candidates.reason: Live testing is a separate browser-driven surface with different setup and verification from automated tests.

candidates.name: Framework quickstart
candidates.reason: The index requires the developer to select a framework, target platform, and language.

required_path.action: Complete Sauce Labs account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Sauce Labs account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Sauce Labs candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The completed job appears in Automated Test Results with its result and assets.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'other', 'requirement': 'Select live or automated testing, web or mobile target, framework, and implementation language. The atomic candidate recorded below selects Selenium with Java.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S2', 'S3']}

{'order': 2, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'A Sauce Labs account is required. The current signup page offers a no-credit-card 28-day trial with 60 automated and live testing minutes and two virtual machines in parallel.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2', 'S4', 'S5', 'S7']}
{'order': 3, 'type': 'software', 'requirement': 'The documented Selenium Java example requires Git, GitHub access, JDK 11 or later, Maven, and an IDE.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S4', 'S8']}

external_gates.description: Email signup requires business email, username, a password meeting five composition rules, a United States or Europe data-center choice, and acceptance of the linked notices.
Test mode, application type, framework, language, target, and data center must be selected.
Automated routes require a Sauce Labs username and access key, with environment variables recommended.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: Completed Selenium job visible in Automated Test Results

excluded.item: Additional browsers, devices, and operating systems
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.
Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Sauce Connect setup unless the selected application is private
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Visual, performance, accessibility, and error-reporting expansion
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

uncertainties.question: Which Sauce Labs product surface, framework, language, and target should define first success?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes substantive steps after account creation, including dependencies, capabilities, application access, execution, wait, and result verification. Steps 3 through 9 record only the documented Selenium Java candidate.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The official documentation is a router and explicitly asks developers to choose a favorite suite; it does not nominate one universal first test.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Sauce Labs show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
